message:
chore(beads): close bd-ljzb (extend conformance harness for extension shapes)

Mark bd-ljzb as closed after completing the shape-aware conformance
harness work:

- Harness now covers all extension shapes: tool, command, provider,
  event hook, UI component, configuration, multi, and general
- Added proper registration introspection via extension_tool_defs()
  and extension_providers() instead of workarounds
- Added 6 new ScenarioExpectation fields for shape-specific assertions
- Updated ext_conformance_scenarios.rs with new scenario kinds
  (flag, shortcut, registration)
- Fixed extension_inclusion.rs score type (u32→f64) and added serde
  aliases for v1/v2 JSON format compatibility

Status change: bd-ljzb in_progress → closed with completion comment
documenting the full scope of changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15n","title":"Update --continue flag to use SQLite index","description":"# Update --continue flag to use SQLite index

## Goal
Use SQLite index for fast lookup of most recent session when --continue is used.

## Current Implementation (src/main.rs)
Currently scans filesystem for most recent:
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let session_dir = sessions_dir_for_cwd(&cwd);
    // Scans all files, sorts by mtime
    let most_recent = fs::read_dir(&session_dir)?
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .max_by_key(|e| e.metadata().ok()?.modified().ok()?);
    // ...
}
```

## New Implementation
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    
    if let Some(meta) = index.find_recent(&cwd)? {
        return load_session(&meta.path);
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem
    // ...
}
```

## Benefits
- O(1) lookup instead of O(n) filesystem scan
- Works across hundreds of sessions instantly
- Consistent with session picker behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: --continue uses index
- Test: --continue falls back to filesystem
- Test: Performance improved

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --continue queries index
- [ ] Fallback works if index empty
- [ ] Faster for users with many sessions","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

 {"id":"bd-167l","title":"Bench: JSONL schema + env fingerprint for extension benchmarks","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical, machine-readable output format for extension benchmark runs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- JSONL record types (per run, per extension, per scenario)
- Environment fingerprint fields:
  - OS/kernel
  - CPU model + core count
  - build profile (debug/release)
  - git commit hash
  - feature flags (ext-conformance, wasm, etc.)
- Timing stats fields:
  - raw samples OR a histogram
  - p50/p95/p99
  - warmup count
- Deterministic formatting + stable key ordering.

# Why
Everything downstream (CI gates, trend tracking, baseline comparisons, docs) depends on this being stable.

# Acceptance
- One end-to-end run emits valid JSONL that validates against the schema.
- Stable ordering across repeated deterministic runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.176631505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:30.828204355Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:30.828039217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","extensions","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-167l","depends_on_id":"bd-20s9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.330087273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1696","title":"Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes","description":"# Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes

## Context
Extensions subscribe to events via pi.on("event_name", handler). For conformance testing, we need to FIRE the same events in both runtimes and compare handler responses.

## Events Defined in pi-mono types.ts
From the ExtensionEvent type union:
- tool_call: {tool, input} -> {allow, message, replace_input}
- tool_result: {tool, result} -> {result} (can modify)
- turn_start: {} -> void
- turn_end: {} -> void
- before_agent_start: {} -> {message, system_prompt}
- input: {content, source} -> {content, skip}
- context: {messages, usage} -> {system_prompt, messages}
- resources_discover: {resources} -> {resources}
- user_bash: {command, result} -> {modified_result}
- session_before_compact: {preparation} -> {should_compact, custom_messages}
- session_before_tree: {options} -> {should_show_tree}

## What To Do
1. For each event type, create 2-3 standard payloads (normal case + edge cases)
2. Save as tests/ext_conformance/fixtures/event_payloads.json
3. Document expected behavior for each event type
4. Include payloads that test: normal flow, blocking behavior, error handling, empty handlers

## Fixture Format
{
  "event_payloads": {
    "tool_call": [
      {
        "name": "basic_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "bash", "input": {"command": "echo hi"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": true}
      },
      {
        "name": "blocked_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "rm", "input": {"path": "/etc/passwd"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": false, "message": "blocked"}
      }
    ]
  }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- All event types from ExtensionEvent have at least 2 payloads
- Payloads are valid JSON matching the TypeScript type definitions
- Edge cases included (empty arrays, null values, unicode, large payloads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.169239280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870081762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870011520Z","close_reason":"Added missing event types (model_select, session_start/switch/fork/compact/tree) and ensured 2+ payloads each; added second session_shutdown payload","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1696","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.185490611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-16kl","title":"Unit: session persistence corruption + edge cases (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Cover session persistence edge cases in `src/session.rs` without mocks, focusing on corruption recovery and branching semantics.

# Why / User Impact
Session files are the backbone of continuity. Corruption recovery, atomic writes, and branch metadata must be robust so users never lose work.

# Scope (Granular)
- Corrupted JSONL lines: verify they are skipped, warnings emitted, and remaining entries load.
- Leaf selection: ensure `leaf_id` points to last valid entry after corruption.
- Branch summaries + compaction summaries: verify serialization, render paths, and round‑trip invariants.
- Session save: verify atomic write behavior and deterministic naming under temp dirs.
- Export path handling: invalid path / permission denied surfaces helpful errors.

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger; capture temp file paths and stderr warnings.
- Record corrupted JSONL fixtures and any exported files as artifacts.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/session_conformance.rs` or a new `tests/session_persistence.rs`.
- No mocks; real filesystem temp dirs only.
- Deterministic, race‑free assertions (avoid wall‑clock coupling).
- Explicit assertions on warning text and recovered message count.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:04.314887696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:00.493559771Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:49:02.096447508Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16kl","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:04.330649898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-16uv","title":"Perf: eliminate per-chunk allocations in SSE stream (SseStream)","description":"# Goal
Reduce CPU + allocation churn on provider streaming path by removing per-chunk String/Vec allocations in src/sse.rs (SseStream UTF-8 handling).

# Why
SSE streaming is on the hot path for all providers. Today SseStream copies bytes into utf8_buffer, then allocates an owned String for valid UTF-8, then copies again into SseParser\x27s buffer. It also allocates a new Vec for trailing bytes when UTF-8 is split across chunks.

# Approach (Extreme Optimization)
- Baseline: add a micro-bench covering SseStream parsing with realistic chunk sizes.
- Optimize: feed &str slices directly from utf8_buffer into SseParser (no intermediate String) and keep trailing bytes in-place (no to_vec).
- Prove: existing SSE unit tests + added chunked UTF-8 regression coverage.

# Acceptance
- [ ] No behavior change (all SSE tests pass)
- [ ] Micro-bench shows improved throughput for chunked inputs
- [ ] Quality gates pass","notes":"## Implementation
- `src/sse.rs`: add a fast-path in `SseStream::poll_next_event` to avoid copying the full incoming chunk into `utf8_buffer` when there is no pending UTF-8 tail.
- Keeps existing behavior for invalid UTF-8 (surface `InvalidData`) and for partial UTF-8 sequences (buffer remainder, continue parsing next chunk).

## Measurement (Criterion)
Command:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust_target_blackfalcon cargo bench --bench tools sse_stream -- --noplot`

Before:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[883.52 us 889.96 us 895.67 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[688.67 us 693.27 us 698.09 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[692.80 us 698.86 us 704.64 us]`

After:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[841.65 us 850.27 us 858.67 us]` (mean ~-4.6% time; p<0.05)
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[676.31 us 689.18 us 700.43 us]` (within noise threshold)
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[682.95 us 688.83 us 698.19 us]` (no change detected)

## Isomorphism Proof
- Ordering preserved: underlying byte stream order is unchanged; only an intermediate copy is removed.
- Delimiters preserved: UTF-8 validation still gates feeding; the same bytes are fed into `SseParser` in the same sequence.
- Error behavior preserved: invalid UTF-8 still returns `InvalidData`.

## Gates
- `cargo fmt --check` OK
- `cargo check --all-targets` OK
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` OK
- `cargo test` OK","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:07:46.482214999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589166154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589138733Z","close_reason":"Completed (SseStream fast-path + bench improvement + gates green)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-kh2.2","title":"E2E RPC: extension session connector scenarios (state/messages/append/set_model)","description":"# Goal
Extend the RPC E2E protocol script (bd-kh2) to cover extension session connector behavior end-to-end.

# Scope
Use a deterministic test extension (JS) that issues a representative session op suite:
- `pi.session('get_state', {})` and assert schema keys
- `pi.session('get_messages', {})` and assert ordering/filtering
- `pi.session('append_message', { message: ... })` and verify persistence
- `pi.session('append_entry', { customType, data })` and verify persistence
- `pi.session('set_name', { name })`
- `pi.session('set_model', { provider, modelId })` and `get_model`
- `pi.session('set_thinking_level', { level })` and `get_thinking_level`
- `pi.session('set_label', { targetId, label })`

# Assertions
- The session API outputs match the spec (bd-321a.1) and are stable across repeated runs.
- Session mutations are persisted according to the spec (verify session JSONL changes).
- Errors use taxonomy-only codes.

# Logging / Artifacts
- JSONL logs per bd-4u9:
  - emitted hostcalls (method=session, op, params_hash)
  - outputs and errors (normalized)
  - timing and redaction summary
- Artifacts:
  - session file before/after (or normalized diff)
  - stdout/stderr transcripts
  - deterministic artifact index + hashes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Session connector RPC E2E runs in CI and fails on drift.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] RPC E2E covers session ops: get_state/messages/entries + mutations + model/thinking
[ ] Persistence verified via session JSONL before/after artifacts
[ ] Outputs stable and taxonomy-correct across repeated runs
[ ] JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 with deterministic index + hashes
[ ] Runs in CI (offline) and fails on drift","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaWolf","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:59.337993784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:22:43.098916941Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:22:43.098810172Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","extensions","rpc","session","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kh2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-321a.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:59.337993784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-kh2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-kh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:59.337993784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":583,"issue_id":"bd-kh2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented 23 E2E RPC session connector tests in tests/rpc_session_connector.rs. Tests cover: get_state (fresh + prepopulated), set_session_name (success + error + overwrite), set_thinking_level (success + missing + invalid), get_messages (empty + with content), get_session_stats (empty + messages + tool calls), get_last_assistant_text (empty + with content), set_model error paths (missing provider/modelId/unknown model), unknown command error, response ID echo, malformed JSON recovery, and full multi-command session lifecycle. All tests use real async RPC server over channels (no VCR needed for session-only ops). Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:21:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lbtm","title":"Unit tests: tools.rs — all 7 built-in tools edge cases","description":"Add/verify unit tests in src/tools.rs for all 7 tools: (1) read: valid file, nonexistent file, offset/limit, binary file detection, image detection, truncation at MAX_LINES/MAX_BYTES. (2) write: create new file, overwrite existing, create parent dirs, write empty file. (3) edit: exact match replace, no match error, ambiguous match error, multi-line replacement, unicode content. (4) bash: simple command, timeout, process tree cleanup, exit codes, stderr capture. (5) grep: regex patterns, context lines, limit, case sensitivity, gitignore respect. (6) find: glob patterns, limit, sorting, gitignore respect. (7) ls: directory listing, limit, trailing slash for dirs. Test real filesystem operations (using tempdir). No mocks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanCat","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:12:50.543107559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:40:05.596240965Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:40:05.596214605Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lbtm","depends_on_id":"bd-2r3s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:17.916113672Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Keep harness generic; per‑extension cases live in fixtures.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:41.014536045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:42:25.252896897Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:41.026977618Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","depends_on_id":"bd-2kyq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:29:54.797285296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":344,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Current conformance harness coverage is focused on tools; extensions add new shapes.

Reasoning: A generalized harness avoids custom per‑extension logic and scales with new types.

Considerations: Keep error messages clear to aid debugging when an extension fails.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:51:42Z"},{"id":383,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"QUALITY GATES: UNIT TESTS + E2E + LOGGING

This harness work is only credible if it is heavily test-covered and produces excellent diagnostics.

Unit tests
- Core harness components must have unit tests:
  - extension load/initialization
  - tool/command invocation plumbing
  - shutdown + cleanup
  - error mapping and normalization

E2E scripts
- Provide at least two E2E modes:
  1) offline/fixture mode (CI-safe, deterministic)
  2) optional live mode (for refresh/debug only)

Logging
- Emit structured JSONL logs with correlation IDs:
  - per extension: load/start/exec/stop events
  - per tool/command: inputs, outputs, timings, redaction summary
  - per failure: classification (missing shim vs policy denial vs nondeterminism)

Artifacts
- On failure, write deterministic artifacts (normalized outputs + diffs) so regressions are unfakable.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:14:45Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ljzb","title":"Extend conformance harness for extension shapes","description":"# Goal
Ensure the test harness can load and exercise each extension type (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates).

# Deliverables
- Harness updates for loading + invoking each shape.
- Standardized test hooks for startup, tool invocation, and shutdown.
- Clear error reporting for incompatible formats.

# Notes
Keep harness generic; per‑extension cases live in fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:41.014536045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:11:25.675102982Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:11:25.675006292Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:41.026977618Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","depends_on_id":"bd-2kyq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:29:54.797285296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":344,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Current conformance harness coverage is focused on tools; extensions add new shapes.

Reasoning: A generalized harness avoids custom per‑extension logic and scales with new types.

Considerations: Keep error messages clear to aid debugging when an extension fails.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:51:42Z"},{"id":383,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"QUALITY GATES: UNIT TESTS + E2E + LOGGING

This harness work is only credible if it is heavily test-covered and produces excellent diagnostics.

Unit tests
- Core harness components must have unit tests:
  - extension load/initialization
  - tool/command invocation plumbing
  - shutdown + cleanup
  - error mapping and normalization

E2E scripts
- Provide at least two E2E modes:
  1) offline/fixture mode (CI-safe, deterministic)
  2) optional live mode (for refresh/debug only)

Logging
- Emit structured JSONL logs with correlation IDs:
  - per extension: load/start/exec/stop events
  - per tool/command: inputs, outputs, timings, redaction summary
  - per failure: classification (missing shim vs policy denial vs nondeterminism)

Artifacts
- On failure, write deterministic artifacts (normalized outputs + diffs) so regressions are unfakable.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:14:45Z"},{"id":675,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picking up bd-ljzb. Will extend conformance harness to cover all extension shapes (tools, commands, providers, event hooks, UI components, configuration, multi, general) with standardized test hooks and JSONL logging.","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:48:47Z"},{"id":676,"issue_id":"bd-ljzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Extended conformance harness for all extension shapes.

Changes:
1. ext_conformance_scenarios.rs:
   - Added 6 new ScenarioExpectation fields: flags_contains, shortcuts_contains, commands_contains, event_hooks_contains, tool_count, flag_count
   - Added registration shape matchers to check_expectations_inner() using manager.list_flags/shortcuts/commands/event_hooks
   - Added 'flag' | 'shortcut' | 'registration' scenario kinds to run_scenario() and run_scenario_with_mocks()
   - Fixed 4 result-type mismatches in returns_contains/action/text_contains/content_types checks
   - Added HostcallKind::Log to interceptor match

2. extension-sample.json: Added scn-plan-mode-003 (registration) and scn-sandbox-004 (registration) scenarios

3. extension_inclusion.rs: Updated InclusionEntry/InclusionList to handle both v1 (binary) and v2 (test generator) JSON formats. score u32→f64, added serde aliases for category names (event-hook, ui, shortcut, basic, etc.)

Tests: All 12 ext_conformance_matrix tests pass. Scenario conformance suite: 20 pass, 3 fail (pre-existing), 2 skip.","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:11:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lqec","title":"Interactive: /fork creates new session file from current branch","description":"# Goal
Bring `/fork` to legacy semantics: create a **new session file** from the current branch and switch to it.

# Legacy Semantics (from `legacy .../docs/tree.md`)
- `/fork` extracts the current branch path into a **new session file**.
- `/tree` changes leaf within the same session file.

# Required Behavior
- When invoked, `/fork` should:
  - choose a fork point (current leaf by default, or selected user message if UI requests)
  - create a new session JSONL with entries representing the selected path
  - set the new session’s leaf appropriately
  - switch the app to the new session

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After `/fork`, subsequent messages append to the new session file.
- [ ] Original session remains unchanged.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:58.151807778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:47.280698171Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:52:06.410341445Z","close_reason":"Completed: /fork creates new session file + prefill editor","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ltk7","title":"PiWasm: Imports/hostcalls + capability enforcement","description":"# Goal
Map JS import objects into wasmtime imports and route side effects through connector hostcalls.

# Scope
- Support JS functions as wasm imports (call ordering deterministic).
- Gate imports by capability manifest and policy (denied => deterministic host_result error).
- Structured logs: compile/instantiate + each imported call boundary (correlation ids).

# Acceptance
- Conformance fixture covers: allowed import, denied import, trap mapping.
- No ambient OS access from wasm.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:27.621347394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:34.468143851Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ltk7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:27.628020812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ltk7","depends_on_id":"bd-3w08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:34.468113174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-m4wc","title":"Extension crash recovery and isolation","description":"Create tests that verify one crashing extension cannot affect other extensions or the host. Requirements: 1. Test: extension throws in register() — host continues, other extensions load 2. Test: extension throws in event handler — event propagation continues to other listeners 3. Test: extension enters infinite loop — watchdog kills it, others unaffected 4. Test: extension exhausts memory — isolated to its context, host survives 5. Test: extension makes invalid hostcall — returns error, doesn't crash host 6. Assert: after crash, host can still load new extensions and dispatch events 7. Assert: crash is logged with extension ID and error details. This is critical for production reliability — one bad community extension must never take down the agent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:10.126352850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:40.162749439Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:40.162667757Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m4wc","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:10.148312866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
